Title: Identification of INOSITOL PHOSPHORYLCERAMIDE SYNTHASE 2 (IPCS2) as a new rate-limiting component in Arabidopsis pathogen entry control

Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane; focal accumulation at fungal penetration sites; SYP61-SV cargo
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proper focal accumulation intensity at pathogen contact sites depends on IPCS2 activity; PEN3 genetically acts in the same pathway as IPCS2 for entry control
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double-mutant genetics with pen3-1; pPEN3:PEN3-GFP imaging; time-course CLSM quantification
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PEN3 focal accumulation occurs at Bgh appressoria. 2. In the ipcs2W205* background, PEN3 focal accumulation frequency and area were unchanged, but fluorescence intensity was significantly reduced. 3. Genetic evidence places IPCS2 in the PEN2/PEN3 pathway rather than the PEN1 pathway.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core cargo/readout of the IPCS2-dependent trafficking phenotype rather than a nutrient metabolism enzyme

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Syntaxin of plants; TGN/secretory vesicle marker
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: TGN; secretory vesicles; focal accumulation at Bgh appressorium tip
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Marks vesicle population proposed to carry IPCS2-derived GIPC-enriched cargo including PEN3 to pathogen entry sites
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SYP61-CFP focal accumulation at fungal contact sites indicates SYP61-secretory vesicles participate in polarized defense secretion. 2. These vesicles are proposed carriers of IPCS2-derived GIPC-dependent cargo.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional role inferred mainly from localization and prior proteomics

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Genetic interaction analysis excluded IPCS2 from the PEN1-dependent pathway
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pen1-1 ipcs2W205* showed additive increase in penetration, unlike pen3-1 ipcs2W205*. 2. This indicates IPCS2 acts outside the PEN1 pathway.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was directly tested to position IPCS2 pathway

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: IPCS2 was genetically assigned to the PEN2/PEN3-dependent pathway
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genetic analysis including pen2-3 pen3-1
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Reference double-mutant behavior of pen2-3 pen3-1 helped interpret IPCS2 pathway placement. 2. IPCS2 likely functions in the PEN2/PEN3 branch of entry control.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as pathway marker rather than direct trafficking cargo

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an inositol phosphate headgroup from phosphatidylinositol to ceramide to form IPC, enabling downstream GIPC synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed step / upstream entry step into GIPC synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor and gateway intermediate for GIPC production
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: trans-Golgi network (TGN); SYP61-secretory vesicle subdomain of the TGN
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Most highly expressed IPCS isoform in all tissues; induced by Bgh infection in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves; upregulated upon Blumeria graminis f. sp. hordei infection
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pUBQ10 constitutive promoter; GPD promoter in yeast complementation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes IPC and Hex-GIPC accumulation and restricts entry of non-adapted powdery mildew fungi; loss of IPCS2 increases ceramide and fungal penetration and reduces PEN3 focal accumulation intensity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Forward genetic screen; mutant analysis (ipcs2W205* and ipcs2-1); qPCR; yeast complementation assay; targeted UPLC-nanoESI-MS/MS sphingolipidomics; transgenic complementation with pUBQ10:IPCS2-mVenus; CLSM; double-mutant genetics
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a new rate-limiting component in pathogen entry control
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Infection with non-adapted powdery mildews Bgh and Erysiphe pisi; adapted powdery mildew Golovinomyces orontii; short-day growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Complex sphingolipids required for pathogen entry control and polarized secretion of defense cargo PEN3
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A knockout-like ipcs2W205* mutant showed strongly increased penetration by non-adapted powdery mildews. 2. Yeast complementation and sphingolipidomics demonstrated that IPCS2 enzymatic activity is required for IPC and Hex-GIPC production. 3. IPCS2 remains at the TGN during infection and is necessary for efficient focal accumulation intensity of PEN3 at fungal entry sites. 4. Genetic analysis places IPCS2 in the PEN2/PEN3-dependent entry control pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to manipulate sphingolipid-mediated defense trafficking, but dosage must be tightly controlled because both loss and constitutive overexpression can cause lesion phenotypes
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes spontaneous lesions at later growth stages, reduced rosette size, ceramide accumulation, and enhanced susceptibility to non-adapted powdery mildew entry; constitutive overexpression also caused spontaneous cell death
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ipcs2-1 has promoter T-DNA insertion with elevated transcript but near wild-type sphingolipid profile; IPCS2 protein levels may be post-transcriptionally stabilized upon infection; IPCS2 function was specific to non-adapted powdery mildews in this study

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Hydroxylated ceramides serving as IPCS2 substrate pool
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates the fatty acid moiety of ceramides, contributing to production of IPCS2 substrate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for IPCS2-dependent IPC/GIPC synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with FAH2 supports production of IPCS2 substrate required for pathogen entry control; fah1 fah2 mutant showed increased Bgh penetration
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAH1 functions upstream of IPCS2 by helping generate hydroxylated ceramide substrates. 2. Loss together with FAH2 increased Bgh penetration, supporting the requirement for IPCS2 products in entry control.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Studied only as fah1 fah2 double mutant; single-gene-specific contribution was not dissected

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Hydroxylated ceramides serving as IPCS2 substrate pool
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates the fatty acid moiety of ceramides, contributing to production of IPCS2 substrate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for IPCS2-dependent IPC/GIPC synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with FAH1 supports production of IPCS2 substrate required for pathogen entry control; fah1 fah2 mutant showed increased Bgh penetration
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAH2 functions upstream of IPCS2 by helping generate hydroxylated ceramide substrates. 2. Loss together with FAH1 increased Bgh penetration, supporting the requirement for IPCS2 products in entry control.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Studied only as fah1 fah2 double mutant; single-gene-specific contribution was not dissected

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Predicted to catalyze IPC formation from ceramide and PI
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms IPC precursor for GIPC synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Weak constitutive expression; about 0.05% of IPCS2 levels in rosette leaves; induced by Bgh infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves; induced by Bgh and more strongly upregulated in ipcs2W205* background
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Minor or compensatory contributor; single knockout was wild-type-like for Bgh penetration and partial knockdown with IPCS2 caused only mild non-significant effect
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single knockout analysis; inducible IPCS1;2 amiRNA knockdown; qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bgh infection; β-estradiol induction of amiRNA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IPCS1 is induced upon Bgh infection and further elevated in ipcs2W205*. 2. IPCS1 likely partially compensates for loss of IPCS2. 3. IPCS1 is not the predominant IPCS isoform for cell entry control.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because experimentally tested in Results as a candidate compensatory IPCS isoform

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Predicted IPC synthase isoform contributing to IPC formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms IPC precursor for GIPC synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High constitutive expression in stems and flowers; inferred specialized role in these tissues
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IPCS3 was discussed as a possible contributor to residual IPC/GIPC production in ipcs2 mutants. 2. No direct functional test was reported in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly functionally validated in Results; included only because the paper identifies it as a homolog potentially contributing to residual IPC/GIPC
